commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Windows doctor/update repair to restart running managed gateways after git updates, detect stale service-version metadata, install only running Windows update repairs, and remove Startup-folder fallback launchers only after Scheduled Task running evidence.

PR surface: Source +99, Tests +278. Total +377 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: Partly: the linked issue has concrete native Windows logs, and current main shows the doctor/update and service-stage paths that explain the failure. This review did not run the exact elevated Windows migration end to end.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Draft/protected maintainer PR should stay in human review until compatibility-sensitive Windows upgrade proof is accepted; no narrow automated repair was identified.

target.title: fix(windows): repair doctor update fallback migration
target.body:
## Summary

- fixes Windows update-mode doctor repair so a stale managed gateway is activated only when the Windows service is already running; stopped services stay staged
- audits stale `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION` values and repairs version drift through the existing gateway service doctor path
- restarts the installed/running managed gateway after an interactive git update, while respecting externally managed service-repair policy
- removes the legacy Windows Startup-folder fallback only after Task Scheduler reports running evidence, including post-update restart migration

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87156.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts` - passed, 151 tests across daemon/cli/commands/auto-reply shards on rebased `b4f59bc4c6e`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-update.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/commands/doctor-service-audit.test-helpers.ts src/daemon/service-audit.ts src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts` - passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/doctor-update.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.ts src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.test.ts src/commands/doctor-service-audit.test-helpers.ts src/daemon/service-audit.ts src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts` - passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings, overall patch correct 0.86
- Fresh Windows probe: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26683741961 - passed on `blacksmith-16vcpu-windows-2025`; native Windows was Microsoft Windows NT 10.0.26100.0 / cmd 10.0.26100.4946, x64, PowerShell 7.4.11, Git 2.51.0.windows.1
- Fresh CI: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26683741960 - in progress; `checks-windows-node-test`, `checks-node-agentic-commands-doctor`, and `build-artifacts` were running with no failures observed when this body was refreshed
- AWS Crabbox daemon-config attempt: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_0b3a68bdf32b`, run `run_c3268096d2c0`; reproduced the full daemon-config no-output timeout, while the explicit touched daemon tests above passed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Windows doctor/update migration now distinguishes running vs stopped managed gateways, detects stale service versions, restarts stale installed/running gateways after update, and only removes the Startup-folder fallback after Task Scheduler running evidence.

Real environment tested: native Windows runner through `windows-testbox-probe.yml` on `blacksmith-16vcpu-windows-2025`; local focused Vitest shards for daemon, CLI update, doctor command, and the CI auto-reply regression fixed by current `origin/main`; AWS Crabbox for remote daemon-config behavior.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `ghx workflow run windows-testbox-probe.yml --repo openclaw/openclaw --ref main -f target_ref=fix/windows-doctor-startup-migration -f runner_label=blacksmith-16vcpu-windows-2025 -f keepalive_minutes=0 -f require_wsl2=false -f import_ubuntu_wsl2=true -f enable_wsl2_features=true`; focused local test/format/lint/autoreview commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: Windows probe run 26683741961 passed and confirmed native Windows execution; focused local tests passed 151 assertions; branch autoreview was clean; WSL2 probe found `wsl.exe`, default version 2, WSL and VirtualMachinePlatform features enabled with exit 0, but distro list/import/exec still failed with exit -1 and `wsl2_ok=false`.

Observed result after fix: the patch is validated for native Windows and the service/doctor unit behavior; WSL2 execution remains blocked by the current runner environment rather than by this patch.

What was not tested: a successful WSL2 distro boot inside the Windows runner, because Ubuntu import/exec failed after feature enablement; the full daemon-config shard, because the remote AWS Crabbox run timed out with no output before touched-test output.
target.author: vincentkoc
